reports.title: A Reproducible CPU-to-GPU Pipeline for Transfer-Entropy Networks: The Econstellar Compute Run behind the Commodity Study
reports.summary: An authored compute-run companion to the commodity transfer-entropy study. It documents how the Econstellar engine estimated directed information-flow networks for 23 commodity futures across eight crisis windows, and what migrating the bottleneck k-nearest-neighbour search from 22 CPU cores to a single GPU bought: bit-identical results (agreeing to within 9e-16), an honest crossover — the CPU wins on the bare estimate, and the GPU only repays its fixed overhead once B=99 surrogate testing inflates the work, by 1.3x to 2.1x as the window lengthens — and two reusable cautions, on JIT compilation cost and on why a block bootstrap silently breaks a k-NN estimator.

reports.title: Econstellar Compute Engine: Verification Run Report, 2026-06-12
reports.summary: We report a complete verification run of the Econstellar compute engine, a continuously deployed econometric service for financial-market analysis in which every public method ships with a pre-registered, failable evaluation. The suite executed 26 method rows — spanning stationarity, unit roots and…
